hero-title: Beginner Path: Start Learning About AI Today
Beginner Path: Start Learning Augmented Intelligence Today
Beginner Path: Start Learning Augmented Intelligence Today
hero-lead: This path is for anyone who is curious about AI and how it can help with things you already do. You do not need any tech know-how. You do not need to know any special words. You do not need to have tried it before. Just come with an open mind and a bit of curiosity.

This path is for anyone curious about AI and how it can help with what you already do. You do not need a tech background, any special words, or any past experience. Just bring your curiosity and an open mind.
This path is for anyone curious about AI and how it can amplify what you already do. No technical background needed, no jargon prerequisites, no prior experience required. Just bring your curiosity.
For anyone curious how AI can amplify what you already do. No technical background, jargon, or prior experience required. Bring curiosity.
For the curious. No background, jargon, or experience needed.
what-intro: Augmented intelligence is not about swapping yourself out for a machine. It is about learning what AI is good at, what it is bad at, and how to put its strengths together with yours. When you do that, you can get things done that neither you nor the machine could do on your own.

Augmented intelligence is not about replacing yourself with a machine. It is about learning what AI does well, what it does poorly, and how to combine its strengths with yours, so together you achieve things neither of you could do alone.

Augmented intelligence is not about replacing yourself with a machine. It is about understanding what AI actually does well, what it does poorly, and how to combine its strengths with yours to achieve outcomes neither of you could manage alone.
Augmented intelligence is not replacement. It is knowing what AI does well, what it does poorly, and combining its strengths with yours for outcomes neither could reach alone.
Not replacement: combine AI's strengths with yours for outcomes neither reaches alone.
what-outcomes: By the time you finish this path, you will understand the big idea behind augmented intelligence. You will know the real things that today's AI cannot do well yet. You will have a clear picture in your head of how people and machines can think together. And you will know enough to decide if the Practitioner Path is a good next step for you.

By the end of this path you will understand the core idea behind augmented intelligence, know the real limits of today's AI, and have a clear mental picture of how people and machines think together. You will also know enough to decide if the Practitioner Path is your next step.

By the end of this path you will understand the core philosophy of augmented intelligence, recognise the real limitations of current AI systems, and have a working mental model for how humans and machines think together. You will also know enough to decide whether the Practitioner Path is your next step.

By the end you will grasp the core philosophy of augmented intelligence, recognise current AI's real limitations, hold a working model of how humans and machines think together, and know whether the Practitioner Path is next.
By the end: the core philosophy, current AI's limits, a working human-machine model, and whether the Practitioner Path follows.
no-prereqs: You do not need to know anything first. This path starts right at the very beginning and builds up bit by bit. Each part points you to the next one. But if something grabs your interest, you can jump straight to it instead.
No prerequisites. This path starts at the very beginning and builds up step by step. Each module links to the next, but you can also jump straight to whatever interests you most.
No prerequisites. This path starts from zero and builds up. Each module links to the next, but you can also jump to whatever interests you most.
No prerequisites. Starts from zero and builds up. Modules link in sequence, or jump to whatever interests you.
No prerequisites. Builds from zero; read in order or jump.
curriculum-intro: Go through these five modules one after the other, in order. Each one builds on the one before it. Step by step, they give you a deeper and deeper understanding of augmented intelligence.
Work through these five modules in order. Each one builds on the last, so step by step you gain a deeper understanding of augmented intelligence.
Work through these five modules in order. Each one builds on the last, giving you a progressively deeper understanding of augmented intelligence.
Work through the five modules in order; each builds on the last for progressively deeper understanding.
Five modules, in order, each building deeper understanding.
module-1-desc: Start here. You will learn what augmented intelligence really means: it is about boosting what people can do by adding machine power, not taking the person out of the picture. You will meet the idea of the digital cyborg, see how artificial intelligence is different from augmented intelligence, and find out why this one difference changes everything.

Start here. You will learn the core idea: augmented intelligence is about amplifying what people can do with machine power, not replacing the person. You will meet the digital cyborg concept, see how artificial and augmented intelligence differ, and why that difference reshapes everything.

Start here. Learn the core definition: augmented intelligence is about amplifying human potential with machine capability, not replacing the human. You will explore the digital cyborg concept, understand the difference between artificial and augmented intelligence, and see why this distinction reshapes everything.

Start here. The core definition: augmented intelligence amplifies human potential with machine capability rather than replacing the human. Covers the digital cyborg concept, artificial versus augmented intelligence, and why the distinction reshapes everything.
Core definition: amplify, not replace. Covers the digital cyborg, artificial versus augmented intelligence, and why it matters.
module-2-desc: Before you lean on AI, you need to know where it falls down. This module looks at how AI sometimes makes things up, how very expensive big models are to run, how it does not really reason the way we do, and the gap between what AI companies say it can do and what it truly does. Knowing these limits is what sets skilled users apart from people who trust it too easily.

Before you rely on AI, you need to know where it breaks. This module covers how it makes things up (hallucination), the huge cost of running big models, its lack of real reasoning, and the gap between what AI companies promise and what the tech delivers. Knowing these limits is what separates skilled users from naive ones.

Before you depend on AI, you need to understand where it breaks. This module covers hallucination, the staggering costs of running large models, the lack of genuine reasoning, and the gap between what AI companies promise and what their technology actually delivers. Understanding these limitations is what separates effective practitioners from naive users.

Before depending on AI, understand where it breaks: hallucination, the staggering cost of large models, the absence of genuine reasoning, and the gap between company promises and delivery. Knowing these limits separates effective practitioners from naive users.
AI's failure modes: hallucination, model cost, no real reasoning, promise-versus-delivery gap. Knowing them separates practitioners from naive users.
module-3-desc: Now that you know what augmented intelligence is and where AI gets things wrong, look at why people and machines working together really matters. This module looks at how modern work keeps getting more complicated, how there is too much information for one person to handle, and why the future belongs to people who learn to think alongside machines instead of trying to beat them.

Now that you know what augmented intelligence is and what AI gets wrong, explore why the human-machine partnership matters. This module looks at the growing complexity of modern work, the problem of too much information, and why the future belongs to people who learn to think alongside machines rather than compete against them.

Now that you know what augmented intelligence is and what AI gets wrong, explore why the human-machine partnership matters. This module examines the growing complexity of modern work, the information overload problem, and why the future belongs to people who learn to think alongside machines rather than compete against them.

Now you know what augmented intelligence is and what AI gets wrong, explore why the partnership matters: the growing complexity of modern work, information overload, and why the future belongs to those who think alongside machines, not against them.
Why the partnership matters: rising work complexity, information overload, and a future for those who think with machines, not against them.
module-4-desc: Theory is helpful, but real examples make it come alive. This module walks you through everyday situations where augmented intelligence changes how work gets done. It shows you how to speed up research, hand off the boring mechanical parts of creative work, help bigger teams work together, and win back time for the kind of thinking that really needs a human brain.

Theory is useful, but examples make it real. This module walks through real situations where augmented intelligence changes daily work: speeding up research, automating the mechanical parts of creative work, helping teams collaborate at scale, and winning back time for the thinking that truly needs a human brain.

Theory is useful, but examples make it real. This module walks through concrete scenarios where augmented intelligence transforms daily work: compressing research cycles, automating the mechanical parts of creative work, scaling collaboration across teams, and reclaiming time for the thinking that actually requires a human brain.

Examples make theory real. This module covers concrete scenarios where augmented intelligence transforms daily work: compressing research cycles, automating mechanical creative tasks, scaling team collaboration, and reclaiming time for thinking that requires a human brain.
Concrete scenarios: compressed research, automated mechanical creative work, scaled collaboration, time reclaimed for human thinking.
module-5-desc: Everything in augmented intelligence rests on three pillars. The first is Logic: the scientific method and clear mathematical thinking. The second is Explanation: building solid explanations that are hard to twist or break. The third is Architected Data Objects: tidy, standard structures that take something complicated and pack it down small. Once you understand these three pillars, you have the base you need for everything in the Practitioner and Researcher paths.

Everything in augmented intelligence rests on three pillars: Logic (the scientific method and mathematical reasoning), Explanation (building durable, hard-to-vary explanations), and Architected Data Objects (standard structures that pack complexity down small). Understanding these pillars gives you the foundation for everything in the Practitioner and Researcher paths.

Everything in augmented intelligence rests on three pillars: Logic (the scientific method and mathematical reasoning), Explanation (creating durable, hard-to-vary explanations), and Architected Data Objects (standardised structures that compress complexity). Understanding these pillars gives you the conceptual foundation for everything that follows in the Practitioner and Researcher paths.

Three pillars underpin augmented intelligence: Logic (scientific method and mathematical reasoning), Explanation (durable, hard-to-vary explanations), and Architected Data Objects (standardised structures that compress complexity). They form the foundation for the Practitioner and Researcher paths.
Three pillars: Logic, Explanation, and Architected Data Objects. The foundation for the Practitioner and Researcher paths.
next-summary: Once you have gone through all five modules, you will have a strong grasp of what augmented intelligence is, why it matters, and the set of ideas it is built on. That is enough to start using these ideas in a loose, everyday way in your own work.

Once you have worked through all five modules, you will have a solid grasp of what augmented intelligence is, why it matters, and the framework it rests on. That is enough to start applying these ideas informally in your own work.

Once you have worked through all five modules, you will have a solid understanding of what augmented intelligence is, why it matters, and the conceptual framework it rests on. That is enough to start applying these ideas informally in your own work.
After all five modules, you will solidly understand what augmented intelligence is, why it matters, and the framework beneath it, enough to start applying these ideas informally in your work.
After five modules: a solid grasp of augmented intelligence and its framework, enough to apply informally.
next-practitioner: If you want to go further and learn the exact methods, tools, and plans for weaving augmented intelligence into your job, the Practitioner Path is made for just that. It picks up right where this path leaves off and builds on everything you have learned here.

If you want to go deeper and learn specific methods, tools, and strategies for weaving augmented intelligence into your professional work, the Practitioner Path is built for exactly that. It builds directly on everything you have learned here.

If you want to go deeper and learn specific frameworks, tools, and strategies for integrating augmented intelligence into your professional workflow, the Practitioner Path is designed exactly for that. It builds directly on everything you have learned here.

To go deeper into specific frameworks, tools, and strategies for integrating augmented intelligence into your professional workflow, the Practitioner Path is designed for exactly that, building directly on everything learned here.
For frameworks, tools, and strategies to integrate it into your workflow, take the Practitioner Path. It builds on this.
continue-cta: Ready to use what you have learned? Carry on to the Practitioner Path for hands-on methods and plans you can put to work.
Ready to apply what you have learned? Move on to the Practitioner Path for hands-on methods and strategies.
Ready to apply what you have learned? Move on to the Practitioner Path for hands-on frameworks and strategies.
